# DRC Ituri Province Ebola Bundibugyo Outbreak — May 2026: Current Case Count & Cumulative Suspected Cases

## Overview

The DRC's **17th Ebola outbreak**, caused by the **Bundibugyo ebolavirus (BDBV)**, was officially declared on **15 May 2026** by the DRC Ministry of Health. This is only the **3rd known outbreak of the Bundibugyo strain** in history. WHO Director-General declared it a **Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC)** on **16 May 2026**.

## Current Case Counts (Most Recent Data)

### As of 20 May 2026 (Project HOPE Situation Report #1):
- **Suspected cases**: 600+
- **Confirmed cases**: 35
- **Deaths**: 139+

### As of 19 May 2026 (CARE report via ReliefWeb):
- **Suspected cases**: 536
- **Probable cases**: 105
- **Confirmed cases**: 34
- **Deaths**: 134

### As of 15 May 2026 (WHO DON602):
- **Suspected cases**: 246
- **Confirmed samples**: 8 of 13 tested positive for Bundibugyo virus
- **Deaths**: 80

## Cumulative Suspected Case Trajectory

The suspected case count shows explosive growth:

| Date | Cumulative Suspected Cases | Source |
|---|---|---|
| 15 May 2026 | 246 | WHO DON602 |
| 19 May 2026 | 536 | CARE via ReliefWeb |
| 19 May 2026 | 500+ | IRC, MSF |
| 20 May 2026 | 600+ | Project HOPE |

The IRC reported a **103% increase in suspected cases within 96 hours** (from ~246 to 500+ between 15–19 May).

## Deaths & Fatality

| Date | Cumulative Deaths | Source |
|---|---|---|
| 15 May 2026 | 80 | WHO DON602 |
| 19 May 2026 | 131–134 | BBC, Al Jazeera, CARE |
| 20 May 2026 | 139+ | Project HOPE |

The crude case fatality rate among suspected cases is approximately **25%** (134/536), consistent with the historical Bundibugyo CFR range of 25–40%.

## Geographic Scope

**DRC – Ituri Province (epicenter):**
- Mongbwalu Health Zone (epicenter; 3 health areas affected)
- Rwampara Health Zone (6 health areas affected)
- Bunia Health Zone (provincial capital)

**DRC – North Kivu Province:** Cases confirmed, including in Goma.

**Uganda:** 2 confirmed imported cases in Kampala (1 death, as of 18 May 2026).

## Key Timeline

- **~29 March 2026**: First signs of epidemic observed (Caritas)
- **24 April 2026**: First suspected case (health worker) develops symptoms
- **5 May 2026**: WHO alerted about unknown illness in Mongbwalu
- **15 May 2026**: INRB confirms Bundibugyo virus; DRC declares outbreak
- **16 May 2026**: WHO declares PHEIC
- **18 May 2026**: U.S. CDC imposes 30-day travel restrictions
- **20 May 2026**: 600+ suspected cases, 139+ deaths reported

## Response

- WHO delivered **11.5 tonnes** of medical supplies within 72 hours and deployed **35+ experts**
- IFRC launched emergency appeal for **CHF 29 million (~USD 34 million)**
- **200 Red Cross volunteers** deployed
- CARE targeting **500,000–1,000,000 people** for assistance

## Data Limitations

All data is sourced from pre-compiled research files in the repository, with the most recent data point from **20 May 2026**. Live search tools were unavailable during this session, so figures from 21–22 May 2026 could not be retrieved.
